titleStmt.title: SII 32, part 1, no. 2: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 2. (A.R. No. 266 of 1991.) EṆṆĀYIRAM, VILLUPURAM TALUK, VILLUPURAM DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the surface of the natural cavern by the side of the Jaina beds in a hilock known as Mēlkuḍalūr-malai

This inscription may be assigned to the early Chōḷa ruler Vijayālaya, not only on the grounds of palaeography but also due to the fact that it is found engraved in continuation of an inscription belonging to the second regnal year of the Pallava king Nṛipatuṅgavarmaṉ (846-872 A.D.)

It records that an individual Tāḻivaikuntaṉ alias Sembiyaṉ Kāvidiyaraiyaṉ, a native of Kuḷattūr in Maḻa-nāḍu had gifted five hundred sheep for burning lamps to the god, (called) Avaṉitilakattu-dēvar at Sēndamaṅgalam in Paṉaiyūr-nāḍu.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī .
kopparakecaripanmaṟku yāṇṭu nālāvatu paṉaiūr nāṭṭuc centama
ṅkalattu Avaṉitilakattut tevaṟku(ti) tiruviḷakkukku maḻanā
ṭṭuk kuḷattūruṭaiyāṉ ṟāḻi vaikuntaṉākiya cempiya
ṉ(r) kāvitiyaraiyaṉ vaitta Āṭaiññūṟu .
Digital edition of SII 32, part 1, no. 2 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
